_description: Wave 192: Episode 0, comedic cold-open. Sets the brand standard.
title: Welcome to the Thicktionary

voiceover.instructions: Deadpan documentary register. Slow, level, slightly weary. Like a 1970s public-broadcaster narrator who has seen too much. No enthusiasm. No upspeak.

segments.name: hugh_cold_open
segments.narration: Thicktionary. Daily catalogue. Objects of unusual girth.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary opener. Three short staccato phrases. Each one its own beat. Like a public radio show intro card.

segments.name: bart
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary. Four words. Stated as bureaucratic fact, not opinion. Tiny falling tone on 'reviewed'.

segments.name: eli
segments.instructions: One word. Deadpan. Slight emphasis. The kind of one-word sentence a documentary narrator drops as a beat.

segments.name: margie
segments.narration: Three times, if the Editor-in-Chief is in a mood.
segments.instructions: Deadpan, completely matter-of-fact. The phrase 'in a mood' should be read with the same flat register as the rest, not winked at. The comedy is in NOT acknowledging the joke.

segments.name: spider
segments.narration: Field correspondence is filed from improbable locations.
segments.instructions: Deadpan documentary. Reads 'improbable locations' as if it's a normal editorial policy, no irony. Slight gravitas on 'field correspondence'.

segments.name: hugh_closer
segments.narration: Strictly things. Never people. It's a documentary obligation.
segments.instructions: Deadpan close. Three short sentences. The final 'It's a documentary obligation' is the punchline but should be read with ZERO smile, like a stated job requirement. Tiny falling cadence on 'obligation'.

segments.name: outro
segments.text:
thiccctionary.com

A new entry each morning
segments.instructions: Deadpan close. Two-word URL like an old public-radio show sign-off. Falling tone on 'dot com'.
